details: Paper 050 compound 1g is 6-chloro-2,2-dimethyl-3-phenyl-1,2-dihydroquinazoline-4(3H)-thione (X=Cl on quinazoline, R=H on phenyl), but row compound_name says 3-(2-chlorophenyl)-2-thioxo-2,3-dihydroquinazolin-4(1H)-one — missing 2,2-dimethyl and places Cl on phenyl instead of quinazoline. Value 218-219 is correct for 1g but compound is misnamed.

Row source_url DOI 10.1002/cbdv.202500394 (Chem & Biodiv) does not match the actual paper containing this compound which is Arch Pharm (Weinheim) DOI 10.1002/ardp.70227 (PMC13006720). The evidence_quote and compound match the paper's compound 10 but the cited DOI/journal is wrong.

Source paper 1990 Yalkowsky 'Melting Point Boiling Point and Symmetry' (DOI 10.1023/A:1015801203702) is in corpus as image-only PDF — pdftotext extracts no text. Evidence_quote 'Table III: 3-nitrotoluene BP 505 MP 288' cannot be verified verbatim.

Same DOI issue as row 63 — DOI 10.1002/cbdv.202500394 does not match actual paper (Arch Pharm, DOI 10.1002/ardp.70227, PMC13006720). The compound 13 quote 'mp 252°C–254°C' is verbatim in the actual paper but the cited DOI/journal is wrong.
Paper 071 PMC12943180, compound 5d is '2-(hydroxy(4-nitrophenyl)methyl)cyclohex-2-en-1-one'; m.p. 135–137 °C verbatim in Section 3.4; DOI 10.3390/molecules31040599 matches; midpoint 136 correct.

Evidence_quote uses ellipsis ('Table 1 Entry Ar R Yield (%) Mp (°C) 2o ... 80') which is a paraphrase. The actual paper row reads '2o p -CH 3 C 6 H 4 (CH 2 ) 4 CH- 25 80'. Value 80°C and compound 2o identity verified but the verbatim quote check fails.

Row compound 2s (6-phenylpyridazin-3(2H)-one, 2s) actually has mp 180–182 °C in paper 011. The cited evidence_quote 'mp = 185–186 °C' belongs to compound 3a (5-Acetyl-2-ethyl-6-phenyl-4-(pyridin-3-ylamino)pyridazin-3(2H)-one), not 2s.
Paper 083 PMC4058676, compound 16a '5-[2-Ethoxyquinazolone-3-yl]-2-phenylthiadiazole'; m.p. 172–173 °C verbatim; DOI 10.3390/ph4071032 matches; midpoint 172.5 correct.

Evidence_quote uses ellipsis '5-ethyl-5-(3-methylbut-2-enyl)barbiturate ... Expt. 158.3' instead of verbatim Mitchell 2008 SI2 row '5-ethyl-5-(3-methylbut-2-enyl)barbiturate tr -2.252 -2.471 158.3 169.0 1.73'. Value/compound correct but quote is paraphrased.
Paper 015 PMC12943364, PB02 'white solid PB02 with a melting point of 205–207 °C' verbatim; DOI 10.3390/molecules31040696 matches; midpoint 206 correct; measured.

Paper 2013 Zhou (J Med Chem) compound 20c quote in paper is 'pale red solid (mp 73-74 °C)' but row evidence_quote uses 'pale red solid (mp 73-74 deg C)' — substitutes 'deg C' for '°C' which is not allowed under whitespace/hyphen-only verbatim tolerance.
Paper Muller 2020 (Materials 13, 4486), Table 5 row for CaCl2·6H2O has Melting Peak 31.7 ± 0.73 °C; quote verbatim; DOI 10.3390/ma13204486 matches; measured by DSC.

Paper 2017 Yalkowsky Table 7 lists mono-nitrobenzene mp as '8' in the Nitro column (tabular format). Row evidence_quote 'Table 7: nitrobenzene MP 8 deg C' is a paraphrased reformat, not verbatim. Value is correct but quote check fails.

Source DOI 10.1016/j.ijpharm.2009.01.026 (Int J Pharm 2009, 373, 24 — Tetko 2009) is NOT in the corpus; only cited in paper 060. Cannot verify the evidence_quote 'Table 1: ximoprofen Melting point (deg C) 178' against the actual source paper.

Paper 009 PMC12943507, compound 5k '3,5-Diamino-4-[(3,4-Dimethoxyphenyl)Diazinyl]-N-Tosyl-1H-Pyrazole-1-Carboxamide ( 5k ) ... Melting Point: 169–170 °C' verbatim; DOI 10.3390/ph19020239 matches; midpoint 169.5 correct. Row compound_name truncates leading '3,5-Diamino-4-[(' but label 5k is unambiguous.

Paper 013 compound 3c full name is '1-(2′-bromo-5-methyl-[1,1′-biphenyl]-2-yl)-2-(4-nitrophenyl)ethan-1-ol'. Row truncates this to '(4-nitrophenyl)ethan-1-ol (3c)' — the standalone compound name is uninterpretable. Quote and value verified but compound name is severely truncated.
Paper 027 PMC9188358, compound 2c 'Propyle 1-hydroxy-1H-indole-2-carboxylate 2c. White solid; mp 170–172 °C' verbatim; DOI 10.1007/s42250-022-00374-9 matches; midpoint 171 correct.

Paper Brown 2000 Table 1 has '3-methylheptane 152.7' in a tabular row, not 'Table 1: 3-methylheptane mp/K 152.7' as in evidence_quote. The quote is a paraphrased reformat. Conversion 152.7 K - 273.15 = -120.45 is correct but quote check fails.

Paper 116 PMC12986091, compound 16 '2-Methyl-2-(4-methylpent-3-en-1-yl)-6-[( E )-(4-nitrophenyl)diazenyl]-7-pentyl-2 H -chromen-5-ol ( 16 ): ... mp 62–65 °C' verbatim; DOI 10.3390/molecules31050796 matches; midpoint 63.5 correct.

Paper 062 PMC3127127 Table 5 row 52 is '2,2-dime-3-pentanol 8.5139 3.0843 136.0 131.1 4.9' (uses abbreviated 'dime'). Row evidence_quote uses ellipsis '...2,2-dimethyl-3-pentanol ... 136.0' which is a paraphrase. Value 136.0 correct but quote check fails.

Row compound 2e (5-Acetyl-2-ethyl-4-(3-methoxyphenylamino)-6-phenylpyridazin-3(2H)-one) actually has mp 158–159 °C in paper 011, not 157–159 °C. The evidence_quote 'mp = 157–159 °C' belongs to compound 2g (3-trifluoromethylphenylamino), not 2e.
Paper 090 PMC6146422, compound 2m '3-(2-furanyl)-6-phenyl-1,2,4-triazolo[3,4-b]-1,3,4-thiadiazole ( 2m ): Yield 53 %; M.p. 203-205°C' verbatim; DOI 10.3390/70800681 matches; midpoint 204 correct.

Confirmed prior flag — paper 011 compound 3e (5-hydroxynaphthalen-1-ylamino) has mp 234–236 °C, but row uses evidence_quote mp 185–186 °C which belongs to compound 3g (4-nitronaphthalen-1-ylamino). Compound 3e in row does not match the cited evidence.

Paper 032 PMC4058664 compound 14a is '[2-(4-Benzyl-2H-phthalazin-1-yl)hydrazino]-6-(4-methoxyphenyl)-pyrimidine-5-carbonitrile' but row compound_name is only '(4-methoxyphenyl)-pyrimidine-5-carbonitrile' — severely truncated and uninterpretable. Quote, value, DOI all verified but compound name is incomplete.

Paper 029 PMC9486790, compound 5g '2-((4-Bromobenzyl)thio)-5-(1-methyl-3-(trifluoromethyl)-1H-pyrazol-4-yl)-1,3,4-oxadiazole ( 5 g ) Mp. 113–114 °C' verbatim; DOI 10.1007/s11164-022-04839-x matches; midpoint 113.5 correct.
